title: Unpacking Protocol: How to Set Up Your Bed and Kitchen in Less Than 3 Hours on Night One
excerpt: Night one unpacking: bed and kitchen in 3 hours, move-in protocol, essentials box, first night survival, Active Movers unpacking tips Addis.
content:
Unpacking protocol: how to set up your bed and kitchen in less than 3 hours on night one

Truck leaves at 6 p.m.; sunrise demands coffee and spine support—not floor sleeping among fifty boxes. Night-one protocol is ruthless priority: bed functional, kitchen drinkable, bathroom operational—everything else waits. Three hours achievable with OPEN FIRST discipline.

This guide sets up bed and kitchen in under three hours on move night one.

Before truck arrives: OPEN FIRST bin

  • Sheets, pillows, one blanket per bed.
  • Kettle, mugs, instant coffee or tea, sugar.
  • Plate, fork, knife, spoon set.
  • Toilet paper, soap, towel.
  • Phone chargers, one lamp bulb.
  • Scissors or box cutter on person—not buried.

Hour 1: bedroom

  1. Clear bed footprint—debris and nails scan.
  2. Frame assemble or place mattress if platform.
  3. Sheets on before exhaustion wins.
  4. Curtains optional—sleep mask acceptable.

Hour 2: kitchen minimum

  1. Counter wipe—even quick.
  2. Kettle and one burner functional if stove connected.
  3. Pantry OPEN FIRST spices for simple meal.
  4. Trash bag hung—unpack generates waste.

Hour 3: bathroom and reset

Shower operational; towels hung; tomorrow clothes accessible. Stop unpacking—shower and sleep.

Crew placement help

Tell movers bedroom and kitchen color boxes into rooms before departure—saves forty minutes hunting.

Children version

Same routine plus favorite toy from OPEN FIRST—bed ritual calms.

What not to do night one

Hang art, assemble library, fight with smart TV—Tuesday problem.

Active Movers night-one tip

Request mattress bag removal at destination—sleep ready faster.

Conclusion

Night-one unpacking protocol: OPEN FIRST bin, bed hour one, kitchen hour two, bathroom hour three—stop at three hours and sleep. Tomorrow unpacks with cognition; tonight survives with bed and tea.
author: Active Movers Team
